mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, mechanically lifting equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of falling FEDFUNDS are not statistically positive (t-stat < 1.65) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, tight labor market conditions signal wage-push inflation and potential Fed tightening, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive or flat in >50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid growth in real GDP combined with rising consumer credit balances indicates unsustainable demand overheating, which triggers inflation expectations and rate hike fears, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 3.5% AND RSAFS monthly growth > 2.0%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances despite high GDP and credit growth.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a divergence where industrial production rises while unemployment remains elevated signals productivity-driven supply expansion, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of instances where INDPRO rises and UNRATE stays above 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and wealth effects, which boost aggregate demand and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months with sequential HOUST increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 2.5% annualized (implied by month-over-month acceleration), inflation fears trigger duration risk repricing, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >50% of instances where CPIAUCSL accelerates above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth coupled with declining unemployment indicates a self-reinforcing labor demand loop that expands corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months where PAYEMS rises >150k and UNRATE falls.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 95.0, supply chain bottlenecks or demand collapse signal earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where INDPRO drops below 95.0.
